claims.claim: The transition from Apple's install-based Core Technology Fee (CTF) to a 5% Core Technology Commission (CTC) on digital revenue in the EU by 2026 is a defensive barrier designed to prevent early-stage utility and OS-layer apps (which monetize through indirect or off-platform models) from surviving on alternative iOS app stores.
claims.why_distinctive: It shifts the framing of the EU fee update from a developer-friendly relaxation of the CTF to an intentional economic defense against utility-based OS wrappers that bypass traditional in-app purchases.
claims.falsifier: Apple waives the 5% CTC for alternative marketplace apps that do not utilize App Store discovery or billing services.
claims.verify: Review Apple's EU Terms Addendum (Section 3.4) and check if CTC applies to transactions initiated outside Apple-hosted systems.

human_input.note: The director would qualify this by noting that Apple's fee restructure effectively prices out high-volume utility applications that attempt to bypass standard Store Services while providing zero direct transactional value to Apple.

claims.claim: Browser extensions (distributed via the Chrome Web Store) under Manifest V3 will serve as the default high-leverage entry point for new client-side OS-layer systems, but will face a structural performance ceiling within 24 months as Google uses security-motivated updates to declarativeNetRequest limits to restrict dynamic UI injection.
claims.why_distinctive: Counters the belief that extensions can permanently serve as fully featured OS-layer clients by predicting a structural policy and api bottleneck enforced by Google.
claims.falsifier: Google introduces new APIs in Manifest V3 or V4 that expand dynamic script execution or custom DOM injection privileges for extension runtimes.
claims.verify: Track Chrome Extensions API updates regarding declarativeNetRequest limits and script injection guidelines from 2026 to 2028.

human_input.note: The director would frame this as a warning that any extension-based client must have a compile-to-native backup plan (e.g., Tauri/Electron client with accessibility overrides) to mitigate sudden browser API restrictions.

claims.claim: Browser-level Extension manifest constraints can be completely bypassed by delegating state management, caching, and network intercept logic to a local unsandboxed Native Messaging Companion Daemon.
claims.why_distinctive: It shifts the architectural paradigm of extensions from thin sandboxed wrappers to standard IPC frontends of full local OS processes.
claims.falsifier: Google deprecates the chrome.runtime.connectNative extension API or restricts message volumes to unusable rates.
claims.verify: Verify extension performance and capability when connecting to a dummy native messaging host under MV3 constraints.

human_input.note: We must ensure the installer handles register paths for the Native Messaging Host correctly across both Windows registry keys and macOS plist locations.

claims.claim: Windows SmartScreen reputation blocks can be programmatically bypassed during the bootstrap phase by utilizing PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest/BITS downloads which do not assign NTFS Zone.Identifier streams, combined with Unblock-File operations.
claims.why_distinctive: It demonstrates how to bypass highly restrictive Authenticode/SmartScreen warnings without buying expensive EV certificates or accumulating download reputation.
claims.falsifier: Microsoft Windows Defender starts treating all files downloaded by PowerShell from public IP spaces with synthetic ZoneId=3 flags at the file system or kernel level.
claims.verify: Run Invoke-WebRequest to download an EXE and inspect its streams using Get-Item -Stream Zone.Identifier.

human_input.note: Even if SmartScreen is bypassed this way, local Antivirus/EDR engines may flag unsigned execution patterns, so binaries must still contain basic metadata and run with minimal privilege escalation requests.
